title: Entering the Promised Land
objective: See God fulfill His promise to Abraham as Israel crosses the Jordan and begins to take the land.

sections.text: Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened, and do not be dismayed, for the LORD your God is with you wherever you go.

title: Joshua's Faithful Leadership
objective: Study Joshua as a model of faith and obedience, and see how God honors His Word.

sections.text: But as for me and my house, we will serve the LORD.

title: Judges — A Cycle of Failure
objective: Recognize the sin-judgment-cry-deliverance cycle in Judges as a picture of humanity's constant need for a rescuer.

sections.text: Then the LORD raised up judges, who saved them out of the hand of those who plundered them.

title: Ruth — A Story of Faithfulness
objective: Discover in Ruth a picture of covenant loyalty (hesed), redemption, and the inclusion of the nations in God's family.

sections.text: But Ruth said, 'Do not urge me to leave you or to return from following you. For where you go I will go, and where you lodge I will lodge. Your people shall be my people, and your God my God.'

title: Samuel & the Demand for a King
objective: Understand Israel's rejection of God as King and God's grace in still working through flawed human institutions.

sections.text: And the LORD said to Samuel, 'Obey the voice of the people in all that they say to you, for they have not rejected you, but they have rejected me from being king over them.'

title: Saul — The People's King
objective: See how Saul's tragic reign reveals that no merely human king can save God's people.

sections.text: And Samuel said, 'Has the LORD as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of the LORD? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to listen than the fat of rams.'

title: David — A Man After God's Heart
objective: Study David as the greatest king of Israel and as a type of Christ, the Son of David who will reign forever.

sections.text: When your days are fulfilled and you lie down with your fathers, I will raise up your offspring after you, who shall come from your body, and I will establish his kingdom. He shall build a house for my name, and I will establish the throne of his kingdom forever.

title: Solomon & the Temple
objective: See the glory of Solomon's reign and temple, and understand why even Israel's greatest king cannot bring final peace.

sections.text: But will God indeed dwell on the earth? Behold, heaven and the highest heaven cannot contain you; how much less this house that I have built!
